title: Texas Tribune/ProPublica: Sole Witness to Martinez Killing Later Died, Families Detail Cover-Up Pattern
excerpt: A joint Texas Tribune and ProPublica investigation revealed that Joshua Orta, the sole eyewitness who disputed the government's account of ICE's killing of U.S. citizen Ruben Ray Martinez, died in a car crash months after witnessing his friend's death — with both families calling out a government pattern of concealment.
content:
Published May 4, 2026, the Texas Tribune and ProPublica investigation provided the most detailed account yet of the circumstances surrounding the killing of Ruben Ray Martinez — the first person killed by ICE during Trump's second term — and its aftermath.

  • Joshua Orta, 25, was in the passenger seat when HSI Supervisory Special Agent Jack Stevens shot Martinez through the open car window at close range in South Padre Island in March 2025. Orta disputed the government's self-defense account, saying the agent had leaned on the hood of the car rather than been struck by it. Orta died in a car crash in February 2026, before he could formally sign a witness statement.
  • Body camera footage reviewed by the Tribune showed officers dragging Martinez's unconscious body from the car and handcuffing it on the roadway; medical attention was not rendered for over a minute after the shooting.
  • The ICE agent was not wearing a body camera, and the relevant footage came only from South Padre Island Police Department cameras — footage that Texas DPS quietly released months later with no formal announcement.
  • A Cameron County grand jury declined to indict the agent. Martinez's mother, Rachel Reyes, said she did not learn an ICE agent had killed her son until nearly a year after his death.
Reyes told reporters: "Immigration agents are there for one purpose only and that's to deport. Not to play cop."

The report documented contradictory commands from officers — at least one agent telling Martinez to "keep going" while another shouted "pull over" — evidence the families argue undermines the government's claim that the shooting was a response to a deliberate failure to comply with lawful orders.
